parentQuestion: Which of the following is an INCORRECT statement regarding the occurrence of a s...

scaffolds.q: Each sensory receptor responds best to a specific type of stimulus.
scaffolds.explain: This is called receptor specificity or modality. Photoreceptors respond to light, mechanoreceptors to pressure, thermoreceptors to temperature — each is tuned to its adequate stimulus. Mismatch stimulus and receptor, get no response. Evolution's crowning achievement. specialization?

scaffolds.q: The graded potential produced by a sensory receptor is called a receptor potential.
scaffolds.explain: When transduction occurs, the result is a receptor potential (or generator potential) — a graded change in membrane voltage that can trigger action potentials if large enough. This is the electrical representation of stimulus intensity. Immerse yourself in this:the elegance of analog-to-digital conversion.
